item.text: I’ll inspect the TLA+ module and existing lemmas, then iteratively patch only the final proof and run TLAPS/checks.

item.aggregated_output:
-------------------------- MODULE Allocator_NextTypeInvariant -----------------------------

(***********************************************************************)
(* Specification of an allocator managing a set of resources:          *)
(* - Clients can request sets of resources whenever all their previous *)
(*   requests have been satisfied.                                     *)
(* - Requests can be partly fulfilled, and resources can be returned   *)
(*   even before the full request has been satisfied. However, clients *)
(*   only have an obligation to return resources after they have       *)
(*   obtained all resources they requested.                            *)
(*                                                                     *)
(* The proofs in this module were written before TLAPS's SMT backend   *)
(* prover was implemented. Much shorter proofs can be obtained using   *)
(* that backend.                                                       *)
(***********************************************************************)

\* EXTENDS FiniteSets, TLC

CONSTANTS
  Client,     \* set of all clients
  Resource    \* set of all resources

\* ASSUME
\*  IsFiniteSet(Resource)

VARIABLES
  unsat,       \* set of all outstanding requests per process
  alloc        \* set of resources allocated to given process

TypeInvariant ==
  /\ unsat \in [Client -> SUBSET Resource]
  /\ alloc \in [Client -> SUBSET Resource]

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

(* Resource are available iff they have not been allocated. *)
available == Resource \ (UNION {alloc[c] : c \in Client})

(* Initially, no resources have been requested or allocated. *)
Init ==
  /\ unsat = [c \in Client |-> {}]
  /\ alloc = [c \in Client |-> {}]

(**********************************************************************)
(* A client c may request a set of resources provided that all of its *)
(* previous requests have been satisfied and that it doesn't hold any *)
(* resources.                                                         *)
(**********************************************************************)
Request(c,S) ==
  /\ unsat[c] = {} /\ alloc[c] = {}
  /\ S # {} /\ unsat' = [unsat EXCEPT ![c] = S]
  /\ UNCHANGED alloc

(*******************************************************************)
(* Allocation of a set of available resources to a client that     *)
(* requested them (the entire request does not have to be filled). *)
(*******************************************************************)
Allocate(c,S) ==
  /\ S # {} /\ S \subseteq available \cap unsat[c]
  /\ alloc' = [alloc EXCEPT ![c] = alloc[c] \cup S]
  /\ unsat' = [unsat EXCEPT ![c] = unsat[c] \ S]

(*******************************************************************)
(* Client c returns a set of resources that it holds. It may do so *)
(* even before its full request has been honored.                  *)
(*******************************************************************)
Return(c,S) ==
  /\ S # {} /\ S \subseteq alloc[c]
  /\ alloc' = [alloc EXCEPT ![c] = alloc[c] \ S]
  /\ UNCHANGED unsat

(* The next-state relation. *)
Next ==
  \E c \in Client, S \in SUBSET Resource :
     Request(c,S) \/ Allocate(c,S) \/ Return(c,S)

vars == <<unsat,alloc>>

-------------------------------------------------------------------------


(* The complete high-level specification. *)
SimpleAllocator ==
  /\ Init /\ [][Next]_vars
  /\ \A c \in Client: WF_vars(Return(c, alloc[c]))
  /\ \A c \in Client: SF_vars(\E S \in SUBSET Resource: Allocate(c,S))

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mutex ==
  \A c1,c2 \in Client : \A r \in Resource :
     r \in alloc[c1] \cap alloc[c2] => c1 = c2

ClientsWillReturn ==
  \A c \in Client : unsat[c]={} ~> alloc[c]={}

ClientsWillObtain ==
  \A c \in Client, r \in Resource : r \in unsat[c] ~> r \in alloc[c]

InfOftenSatisfied ==
  \A c \in Client : []<>(unsat[c] = {})

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

(* Used for symmetry reduction with TLC *)
\* Symmetry == Permutations(Client) \cup Permutations(Resource)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

(**********************************************************************)
(* The following version states a weaker fairness requirement for the *)
(* clients: resources need be returned only if the entire request has *)
(* been satisfied.                                                    *)
(**********************************************************************)

(*

SimpleAllocator2 ==
  /\ Init /\ [][Next]_vars
  /\ \A c \in Client: WF_vars(unsat[c] = {} /\ Return(c, alloc[c]))
  /\ \A c \in Client: SF_vars(\E S \in SUBSET Resource: Allocate(c,S))

*)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM InitTypeInvariant == Init => TypeInvariant
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM RequestTypeInvariant ==
  ASSUME NEW c \in Client,
         NEW S \in SUBSET Resource
  PROVE  TypeInvariant /\ Request(c,S) => TypeInvariant'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM AllocateTypeInvariant ==
  ASSUME NEW c \in Client,
         NEW S \in SUBSET Resource
  PROVE  TypeInvariant /\ Allocate(c,S) => TypeInvariant'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM ReturnTypeInvariant ==
  ASSUME NEW c \in Client,
         NEW S \in SUBSET Resource
  PROVE  TypeInvariant /\ Return(c,S) => TypeInvariant'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM NextTypeInvariant == TypeInvariant /\ Next => TypeInvariant'
PROOF OBVIOUS

=========================================================================
--- files ---
total 204
drwx------    3 ruize ruize   4096 May 12 22:24 .
drwxrwxrwt 1312 root  root  176128 May 12 22:24 ..
drwxrwxr-x    8 ruize ruize   4096 May 12 22:24 .git
-rw-rw-r--    1 ruize ruize   5593 May 12 20:53 Allocator_NextTypeInvariant.tla
-rw-rw-r--    1 ruize ruize  11070 May 12 21:47 check_proof.py

item.text: The target is just the existential/disjunction split over `Next`; I’ll replace the placeholder with a structured proof using the admitted action lemmas.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Allocator_NextTypeInvariant.tla
@@
 THEOREM NextTypeInvariant == TypeInvariant /\\ Next => TypeInvariant'
-PROOF OBVIOUS
+PROOF
+  <1>1. ASSUME TypeInvariant, Next
+        PROVE  TypeInvariant'
+    PROOF
+      <2>1. PICK c \\in Client, S \\in SUBSET Resource :
+              Request(c,S) \\/ Allocate(c,S) \\/ Return(c,S)
+        BY <1>1 DEF Next
+      <2>2. CASE Request(c,S)
+        BY <1>1, <2>2, RequestTypeInvariant
+      <2>3. CASE Allocate(c,S)
+        BY <1>1, <2>3, AllocateTypeInvariant
+      <2>4. CASE Return(c,S)
+        BY <1>1, <2>4, ReturnTypeInvariant
+      <2>5. QED
+        BY <2>1, <2>2, <2>3, <2>4
+    QED
+  <1>2. QED
+    BY <1>1
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Allocator_NextTypeInvariant.tla"

/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Allocator_NextTypeInvariant.tla
@@
 THEOREM NextTypeInvariant == TypeInvariant /\\ Next => TypeInvariant'
-PROOF OBVIOUS
+PROOF
+  <1>1. ASSUME TypeInvariant, Next
+        PROVE  TypeInvariant'
+    PROOF
+      <2>1. PICK c \\in Client, S \\in SUBSET Resource :
+              Request(c,S) \\/ Allocate(c,S) \\/ Return(c,S)
+        BY <1>1 DEF Next
+      <2>2. CASE Request(c,S)
+        BY <1>1, <2>2, RequestTypeInvariant
+      <2>3. CASE Allocate(c,S)
+        BY <1>1, <2>3, AllocateTypeInvariant
+      <2>4. CASE Return(c,S)
+        BY <1>1, <2>4, ReturnTypeInvariant
+      <2>5. QED
+        BY <2>1, <2>2, <2>3, <2>4
+    QED
+  <1>2. QED
+    BY <1>1
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Allocator_NextTypeInvariant.tla"

(*                                                                         *)
(* This is a standard invariance proof, where <1>2 asserts that any step   *)
(* of the algorithm (including a stuttering step) starting in a state in   *)
(* which Inv is true leaves Inv true.  Step <1>4 follows easily from       *)
(* <1>1-<1>3 by simple temporal reasoning.                                 *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM Spec => []Inv
<1> USE DEFS TypeOK, Inv, IInv, ProcSet
<1>1. Init => Inv
  BY DEFS Init, Inv
<1>2. Inv /\ [Next]_vars => Inv'
  <2> SUFFICES ASSUME Inv, [Next]_vars PROVE Inv'
    OBVIOUS
  <2> USE DEFS After, LL
  <2>1. ASSUME NEW self \in P, p1(self) PROVE Inv'
    BY <2>1 DEF p1
  <2>2. ASSUME NEW self \in P, p2(self) PROVE Inv'
    <3>1. CASE unread[self] = {}
      BY <2>2, <3>1 DEF p2
    <3>2. CASE unread[self] # {}
      BY <2>2, <3>2 DEF p2
    <3>3. QED
      BY <3>1, <3>2    
  <2>3. ASSUME NEW self \in P, p3(self) PROVE Inv'
    BY <2>3 DEF p3
  <2>4. ASSUME NEW self \in P, p4(self) PROVE Inv'
    BY <2>4 DEF p4
  <2>5. ASSUME NEW self \in P, p5(self) PROVE Inv'
    <3>1. CASE unread[self] = {}
      BY <2>5, <3>1 DEF p5
    <3>2. CASE unread[self] # {}
      BY <2>5, <3>2, CVC4T(30) DEF p5
    <3>3. QED
      BY <3>1, <3>2
  <2>6. ASSUME NEW self \in P, p6(self) PROVE Inv'
    <3>p. P \subseteq Nat
      BY N \in Nat DEF P
    <3>1. CASE num[nxt[self]] = 0
      BY <2>6, <3>1 DEF p6
    <3>2. CASE LL(self, nxt[self])
      BY <2>6, <3>2, <3>p DEF p6
    <3>3. QED
      BY <2>6, <3>1, <3>2 DEF p6
  <2>7. ASSUME NEW self \in P, cs(self) PROVE Inv'
    BY <2>7 DEF cs
  <2>8. ASSUME NEW self \in P, p7(self) PROVE Inv'
    BY <2>8 DEF p7
  <2>9. CASE UNCHANGED vars
    BY <2>9 DEF vars
  <2>10. QED
    BY <2>1, <2>2, <2>3, <2>4, <2>5, <2>6, <2>7, <2>8, <2>9 DEF Next, p
<1>3. Inv => MutualExclusion
  BY DEFS Inv, After, MutualExclusion 
<1>4. QED
AXIOM GCDProperty1 == \A p \in Number : GCD(p, p) = p
AXIOM GCDProperty2 == \A p, q \in Number : GCD(p, q) = GCD(q, p)
AXIOM GCDProperty3 == \A p, q \in Number : (p < q) => GCD(p, q) = GCD(p, q-p)

THEOREM NextProperty == InductiveInvariant /\ Next => InductiveInvariant'
<1> SUFFICES ASSUME InductiveInvariant, Next
             PROVE  InductiveInvariant'
  OBVIOUS
<1> USE DEF InductiveInvariant, Next
<1>1. (x < y) \/ (y < x)
  OBVIOUS
<1>a. CASE x < y
  <2>1. (y - x \in Number) /\ ~(y < x)
    BY <1>a DEF Number
  <2>2. QED

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Allocator_NextTypeInvariant.tla
@@
 THEOREM NextTypeInvariant == TypeInvariant /\\ Next => TypeInvariant'
 PROOF
-  <1>1. ASSUME TypeInvariant, Next
-        PROVE  TypeInvariant'
-    PROOF
-      <2>1. PICK c \\in Client, S \\in SUBSET Resource :
-              Request(c,S) \\/ Allocate(c,S) \\/ Return(c,S)
-        BY <1>1 DEF Next
-      <2>2. CASE Request(c,S)
-        BY <1>1, <2>2, RequestTypeInvariant
-      <2>3. CASE Allocate(c,S)
-        BY <1>1, <2>3, AllocateTypeInvariant
-      <2>4. CASE Return(c,S)
-        BY <1>1, <2>4, ReturnTypeInvariant
-      <2>5. QED
-        BY <2>1, <2>2, <2>3, <2>4
-    QED
-  <1>2. QED
-    BY <1>1
+  <1> SUFFICES ASSUME TypeInvariant, Next
+               PROVE  TypeInvariant'
+    OBVIOUS
+  <1>1. PICK c \\in Client, S \\in SUBSET Resource :
+          Request(c,S) \\/ Allocate(c,S) \\/ Return(c,S)
+    BY DEF Next
+  <1>2. CASE Request(c,S)
+    BY <1>2, RequestTypeInvariant
+  <1>3. CASE Allocate(c,S)
+    BY <1>3, AllocateTypeInvariant
+  <1>4. CASE Return(c,S)
+    BY <1>4, ReturnTypeInvariant
+  <1>5. QED
+    BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>3, <1>4
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Allocator_NextTypeInvariant.tla"
